title: Davallodes multidentata
intro: Davallodes multidentata, also known as the 'multidental davallia', is a species of fern native to tropical regions of Asia. The health benefits of Davallodes multidentata include its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Davallodes multidentata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Davallodes multidentata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of extracts and capsules, to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Davallodes multidentata is classified within the family Davalliaceae and is a species of fern. It belongs to the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. Further, it is ranked as a genus within the family Davalliaceae, being a member of the Davalliaceae family. The classification of Davallodes multidentata also includes its kingdom as Plantae and the phylum as Pteridophyta. It is also classified as a type of vascular plant within the kingdom Plantae.
